# Saikuro

> Generated by docmd

  Shared wire format and runtime behavior for all adapters

  How Saikuro works: runtime, adapters, schema, transports, and protocol

  Install Saikuro adapters and the runtime
- [Quick Start](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/getting-started/quickstart/)
  Get two languages talking with Saikuro in under 10 minutes
- [Code Generation](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/codegen/)
  Generate typed client bindings from a Saikuro schema
- [Commands](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/commands/)
  Saikuro development commands via the just runner
- [Error Handling](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/errors/)
  How Saikuro surfaces and handles errors across languages
- [Examples](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/examples/)
  Cross-language patterns and real-world usage
- [Invocation Primitives](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/invocations/)
  The six ways to communicate across language boundaries with Saikuro
- [Logging](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/logging/)
  Structured logging through the Saikuro runtime
- [Schema](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/schema/)
  Declare your functions, types, capabilities, and namespaces
- [Storage](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/storage/)
  Platform-agnostic storage backends for key-value and file operations
- [Transports](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/transports/)
  In-memory, Unix sockets, TCP, WebSocket, and WasmHost transport options
- [WASM](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/guide/wasm/)
  Run Saikuro entirely in the browser via WebAssembly
- [Saikuro](https://nisoku.org/Saikuro/docs/)
  Cross-language invocation fabric for seamless multi-runtime integration
